title: Humboltia amoena

intro: Humboldtia amoena, also known as the 'Indian poison tree', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and seeds that contain a toxic alkaloid. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
